UNIVERSITY EXAM - PREVIOUS YEAR QUESTION PAPER
Subject: Object Oriented Programming Using Java
Year: 2022
Max Marks: 100

PART A (Answer all questions)
1. Explain the difference between checked and unchecked exceptions. (3 marks)
2. What is a constructor? Can it be overloaded? (3 marks)
3. Write the syntax for a multi-catch block. (3 marks)
4. What is the purpose of the 'super' keyword? (3 marks)

PART B
5. Write a Java program to demonstrate user-defined exception handling. (9 marks)
6. Explain method overriding with a suitable example program. (9 marks)
7. What are the different types of inheritance in Java? (9 marks)
8. Discuss the differences between throw and throws keywords. (9 marks)

---

UNIVERSITY EXAM - PREVIOUS YEAR QUESTION PAPER
Subject: Object Oriented Programming Using Java
Year: 2023
Max Marks: 100

PART A (Answer all questions)
1. Differentiate between checked and unchecked exceptions with examples. (3 marks)
2. Explain the concept of data encapsulation. (3 marks)
3. Can we catch multiple exceptions in a single catch block? Explain. (3 marks)
4. Define abstract class. (3 marks)

PART B
5. Write a program to create a custom exception called InvalidAgeException. (9 marks)
6. Illustrate the concept of interfaces with an example. (9 marks)
7. Differentiate between throw and throws. (9 marks)
8. Explain the lifecycle of a thread in Java. (9 marks)
